grid_dims_observed.note: x_chain inner segments [4.00, 2.00, 3.00] match master grid exactly: 1(0.0)-2(4.0)-3(6.0)-4(9.0); overall '9.00' bracket printed above spans 1-4. Leading 0.60/trailing 0.50 are eave overhangs outside the 1-4 structural span. y_chain [2.00, 3.70, 2.20, 3.70, 1.80] = F-E, E-D, D-C, C-B, B-A giving F(13.40)/E(11.40)/D(7.70)/C(5.50)/B(1.80)/A(0.0), matching the master grid exactly. The leading 1.00 above F is a roof/eave dimension outside the F-A span. IMPORTANT CROSS-VALIDATION: the trailing 2.05m dimension below grid A matches the master grid file's dummy row 'Aprime' (pos_m -2.05, previously flagged 'provisional_ground_floor_only' / 'not_yet_cross_checked_against_architecture_or_roof_plan') EXACTLY -- this ground-floor electrical/architecture sheet confirms the building envelope genuinely extends 2.05m south of grid A on this floor (the ทางเดิน/walkway + part of เฉลียง/porch + SB bell-switch location shown at the bottom of this plan sit within that 2.05m strip). This is real supporting evidence for the master file's dummy grid, though the master file itself is not edited here per instructions.

2-gang switch cluster labeled 'h,i' (two adjacent filled dots) mounted on the wall opening between the corridor and ครัว/เตรียมอาหาร, controlling circuits h (ครัว) and i

elements.description: Single switch (small rectangular double-bar glyph, distinct from the standard dot/hook glyphs on the EE-01 legend) labeled 'i', mounted on the top wall of ซักล้าง near its door threshold

Small filled dot labeled 'd' at the door threshold between the corridor and ครัว -- appears to be a wiring junction/drop point for circuit d rather than a separate fixture (no ⊕ downlight glyph drawn there, unlike the 4 'd' fixtures below in ทานอาหาร)
Downlight (⊕) labeled 'g', positioned at the wall opening into เตรียมอาหาร directly below the 'h,i' switch cluster, feeding into 2 more 'g' downlights inside the room (3 total in this run)
elements.room: เตรียมอาหาร

elements.description: 4 downlights (⊕) labeled 'd', arranged in 2 rows of 2 (left and right columns) spanning the length of ทานอาหาร
2 downlights (⊕) labeled 'c' in a single row in โถง (hall), left and center-right positions
Downlight (⊕) labeled 'e' at the wall boundary between เตรียมอาหาร/corridor and ห้องน้ำ 1, feeding horizontally into the 2 'f' bathroom fixtures

elements.description: 2 downlights (⊕) labeled 'f' inside ห้องน้ำ 1, one near the sink/mirror area, one near the toilet
Downlight (⊕) labeled 'e', in line with the 'c' row in โถง, on the same vertical wire run as the other 2 'e' fixtures

Downlight (⊕) labeled 'e' at the stair-landing corridor, at the point where the '1/LP' feed arrow (panel LP circuit 1) rises from below -- marks this run as the panel feed point for the lighting circuit, while still itself being a fixture

A dot cluster labeled 'e' plus an adjacent small glyph with a hooked/tailed stroke (resembles the EE-01 legend's two-way-switch symbol) near the top of the staircase -- could not confidently resolve whether this is a lettered load label ('l'/'1') or an unlabeled two-way switch icon
2-gang switch cluster labeled 'j,k' (filled dots) near the bottom of the staircase, at the wall opening toward เก็บของ/จอดรถ -- controls circuit j and circuit k (the จอดรถ outdoor wall lamp)

Single switch (same rectangular double-bar glyph as 'i') labeled 'j', mounted on the wall at the bottom of the under-stair area near the door/opening leading toward เก็บของ's landing -- likely a second switching location for circuit j (2-way control), not cross-confirmed against a specific fixture inside เก็บของ itself
elements.room: under-stair area near เก็บของ

3-gang switch cluster labeled 'a,b,c' at the wall opening between โถง/staircase and พักผ่อน, near a wall-mounted bell/buzzer glyph boxed 'B' -- controls circuits a (เฉลียง), b (พักผ่อน), c (โถง)
elements.room: โถง / พักผ่อน wall opening

elements.description: 4 downlights (⊕) labeled 'b', arranged in 2 rows of 2 spanning พักผ่อน (living/relax room)
2 downlights (⊕) labeled 'a' in a single row in เฉลียง (porch)
Incoming service run at the bottom of the sheet: '2-2.5 NYY-ø25mm.uPVC' wire down to a weatherproof bell switch (SB, circled) near the entrance/ทางเดิน, at the ±0.00 reference level line

Boxed architectural floor-level markers '3 | +0.50 | (2)' (near ซักล้าง and again near ห้องน้ำ 1) and '4 | +0.60 | (5)' (near เก็บของ) -- these are floor-level/elevation callouts carried over from the architectural base drawing, not electrical symbols; excluded from elements list, consistent with the analogous marker excluded on house #4's equivalent EE-03 sheet

warnings: GRID: this sheet's independently-read grid (columns 1/2/3/4, rows F/E/D/C/B/A) matches the master grid file (บ้าน_เล็ก_2ชั้น_03_หน้า00_gridline.json) exactly for all named lines, cross-validating both extractions. Additionally, the trailing 2.05m dimension south of grid A on this sheet matches the master's dummy row 'Aprime' (-2.05m) exactly -- see grid_dims_observed note for detail; this is new supporting evidence the master file did not have when it was written (master flagged this dummy row as 'provisional' / 'not yet cross-checked against architecture').

This is a 2-story house so this sheet only covers the ground floor; the corresponding upper-floor lighting plan is EE-04 (page 62, separate file). Only one panel-feed tag ('1/LP') appears on this floor -- expect '2/LP' on the upper floor, following the same convention seen in house #4.

Two distinct switch glyphs are used on this sheet: a solid dot / hooked-dot cluster (matches the EE-01 legend's 1-way/2-way switch symbols) for multi-gang clusters (h,i / j,k / a,b,c), and a separate small rectangular double-bar glyph (labels 'i' near ซักล้าง and 'j' near เก็บของ) that does not exactly match either legend switch glyph -- read as single switches by position/context, flagged for human review since the glyph itself is ambiguous.

The distinction between a full ⊕ (crossed circle = downlight fixture) and a plain filled dot (= wire junction, no fixture) was used to separate real fixture counts from wiring path markers -- e.g. the 'd' dot at the ครัว doorway threshold was read as a junction (not a 5th 'd' fixture) because it lacks the ⊕ cross, unlike the 4 confirmed 'd' downlights in ทานอาหาร. This convention was not independently confirmed against a legend entry (the EE-01 legend does not explicitly define a bare-dot 'junction' symbol) -- treat as an inference, not a certainty.

No fixture symbol was confidently identified inside เก็บของ (storage) itself -- only a wall switch ('j') on its boundary wall; possibly an unlit/unlabeled space or a fixture too faint to resolve, consistent with the same room type lacking a clear fixture on house #4's equivalent sheet.

This house's panel schedule (EE-02, page 60 in this house's own set) is a fresh independent read, not yet cross-checked here for circuit-position alignment with the '1/LP' tag seen on this sheet -- position 1 on EE-02 is labeled load 'แสงสว่าง' (lighting), consistent with this being the ground-floor lighting circuit feed.
